testcase.name: maxCoursesTest
testcase.system-out: Calling new Degree( ) Reading the value of private static final int MAX_COURSES ~> 50 ✓

testcase.name: countTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Degree( ) Reading the value of private int count ~> 0 ✓ Calling new Course( "earqktdjfy", 48824, 'A', 1, 1, 41.0, true ) Calling new StudentCourse( [48824A (41.00 cr), "earqktdjfy". testcase.name: titleOfThesisTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Degree( ) Reading the value of private String titleOfThesis ~> "No title" Verifying that "No title" matches the expected pattern ""?No\s+title"?" ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private String titleOfThesis ~> "No title" Checking whether getTitleOfThesis( ) returns a String matching "No title" ~> "No title" (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private String titleOfThesis ~> "No title" Calling setTitleOfThesis( "jhugqwue pxjjwgcd" ) Checking whether the value of private String titleOfThesis matches "jhugqwue pxjjwgcd" ~> "jhugqwue pxjjwgcd" (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private String titleOfThesis ~> "jhugqwue pxjjwgcd" Checking whether getTitleOfThesis( ) returns a String matching "jhugqwue pxjjwgcd" ~> "jhugqwue pxjjwgcd" (true) ✓ Calling setTitleOfThesis( null ) Calling getTitleOfThesis( ) ✓ Reading the value of private String titleOfThesis ~> "jhugqwue pxjjwgcd" ✓

Mandatory, period: 1\.?\] Year: 2023, Grade: 2\.?\] " Attempting to locate the starting point of the regular expression pattern: "\[?\[6167" ✓ ✓

testcase.name: myCoursesTests
testcase.system-out:
Calling new Degree( ) Reading the value of private dev.m3s.programming2.homework2.StudentCourse[] myCourses ~> [null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null] Verifying that the default array length is 0... ✓ Calling new Course( "knxjfrkmvp", 240997, 'P', 1, 3, 16.0, true ) Calling new StudentCourse( [240997P (16.00 cr), "knxjfrkmvp". Mandatory, period: 3.], 2, 2026 ) Calling addStudentCourse( [240997P (16.00 cr), "knxjfrkmvp". Mandatory, period: 3.] Year: 2026, Grade: 2.] ) Calling getCourses( ) Accessing getCourses()[0]... Checking whether the two student course objects are equal: => StudentCourse 2: Reading the value of private Course course ~> [240997P (16.00 cr), "knxjfrkmvp". Mandatory, period: 3.] => StudentCourse 1: Reading the value of private Course course ~> [240997P (16.00 cr), "knxjfrkmvp". Mandatory, period: 3.] Checking whether the two course objects are equal: => Course 2: Reading the value of private String name ~> "knxjfrkmvp" => Course 1: Checking whether private String name is "knxjfrkmvp" ~> "knxjfrkmvp" (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "240997P" => Course 1: Checking whether the value of private String courseCode matches "240997P" ~> "240997P" (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private Character courseBase ~> 'P' => Course 1: Checking whether private Character courseBase is 'P' ~> 'P' (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private int period ~> 3 => Course 1: Checking whether private int period is 3 ~> 3 (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private double credits ~> 16.0 => Course 1: Checking whether private double credits is 16.0 ~> 16.0 (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private boolean numericGrade ~> true => Course 1: Checking whether private boolean numericGrade is true ~> true (true) ✓ => The course objects are equal.
=> StudentCourse 2: Reading the value of private int gradeNum ~> 2 => StudentCourse 1: Checking whether private int gradeNum is 2 ~> 2 (true) ✓ => StudentCourse 2: Reading the value of private int yearCompleted ~> 2026 => StudentCourse 1: Checking whether private int yearCompleted is 2026 ~> 2026 (true) ✓ => The student course objects are equal.
Reading the value of private dev.m3s.programming2.homework2.StudentCourse[] myCourses ~> [[240997P (16.00 cr), "knxjfrkmvp". Mandatory, period: 3.] Year: 2026, Grade: 2.], null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null, null] Accessing myCourses[0]... Checking whether the two student course objects are equal: => StudentCourse 2: Reading the value of private Course course ~> [240997P (16.00 cr), "knxjfrkmvp". Mandatory, period: 3.] => StudentCourse 1: Reading the value of private Course course ~> [240997P (16.00 cr), "knxjfrkmvp". Mandatory, period: 3.] Checking whether the two course objects are equal: => Course 2: Reading the value of private String name ~> "knxjfrkmvp" => Course 1: Checking whether private String name is "knxjfrkmvp" ~> "knxjfrkmvp" (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "240997P" => Course 1: Checking whether the value of private String courseCode matches "240997P" ~> "240997P" (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private Character courseBase ~> 'P' => Course 1: Checking whether private Character courseBase is 'P' ~> 'P' (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private int period ~> 3 => Course 1: Checking whether private int period is 3 ~> 3 (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private double credits ~> 16.0 => Course 1: Checking whether private double credits is 16.0 ~> 16.0 (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private boolean numericGrade ~> true => Course 1: Checking whether private boolean numericGrade is true ~> true (true) ✓ => The course objects are equal.
=> StudentCourse 2: Reading the value of private int gradeNum ~> 2 => StudentCourse 1: Checking whether private int gradeNum is 2 ~> 2 (true) ✓ => StudentCourse 2: Reading the value of private int yearCompleted ~> 2026 => StudentCourse 1: Checking whether private int yearCompleted is 2026 ~> 2026 (true) ✓ => The student course objects are equal.
Initializing a new array called 'newCourses' with 4 random StudentCourses: Calling new Course( "vwnwdcjpoj", 911627, 'S', 0, 5, 3.0, true ) Calling new StudentCourse( [911627S ( 3.00 cr), "vwnwdcjpoj". Optional, period: 5.], 0, 2007 ) Calling new Course( "kllrknjxoo", 300754, 'S', 1, 3, 48.0, false ) Calling new StudentCourse( [300754S (48.00 cr), "kllrknjxoo". Mandatory, period: 3.], 70, 2004 ) Calling new Course( "ofwfberafs", 189318, 'A', 1, 3, 32.0, false ) Calling new StudentCourse( [189318A (32.00 cr), "ofwfberafs". Mandatory, period: 3.], 70, 2015 ) Calling new Course( "kxwcckcbso", 116744, 'S', 0, 4, 14.0, false ) Calling new StudentCourse( [116744S (14.00 cr), "kxwcckcbso". Optional, period: 4.], 65, 2005 ) Adding the contents of the newCourses array to the degree using addStudentCourses method... Calling addStudentCourses( [911627S ( 3.00 cr), "vwnwdcjpoj". Optional, period: 5.] Year: 2007, Grade: "Not graded".], [300754S (48.00 cr), "kllrknjxoo". Mandatory, period: 3.] Year: 2004, Grade: F.], [189318A (32.00 cr), "ofwfberafs". Mandatory, period: 3.] Year: 2015, Grade: F.], [116744S (14.00 cr), "kxwcckcbso". Optional, period: 4.] Year: 2005, Grade: A.] ) Calling getCourses( ) Each object in the newCourses array should now match the object at the next index in the myCourses array. Comparing values newCourses[0] and getCourses()[1] Checking whether the two student course objects are equal: => StudentCourse 2: Reading the value of private Course course ~> [911627S ( 3.00 cr), "vwnwdcjpoj". Optional, period: 5.] => StudentCourse 1: Reading the value of private Course course ~> [911627S ( 3.00 cr), "vwnwdcjpoj". testcase.name: degreeTitleTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Degree( ) Reading the value of private String degreeTitle ~> "No title" Verifying that "No title" matches the expected pattern ""?No\s+title"?" ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private String degreeTitle ~> "No title" Checking whether getDegreeTitle( ) returns a String matching "No title" ~> "No title" (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private String degreeTitle ~> "No title" Calling setDegreeTitle( "kvtbieyh bdluiczk" ) Checking whether the value of private String degreeTitle matches "kvtbieyh bdluiczk" ~> "kvtbieyh bdluiczk" (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private String degreeTitle ~> "kvtbieyh bdluiczk" Checking whether getDegreeTitle( ) returns a String matching "kvtbieyh bdluiczk" ~> "kvtbieyh bdluiczk" (true) ✓ Calling setDegreeTitle( null ) Calling getDegreeTitle( ) ✓ Reading the value of private String degreeTitle ~> "kvtbieyh bdluiczk" ✓
